evals.prompt: Add a custom dropdown menu component in React. The user clicks a button to open it and can select options with keyboard arrows.
evals.expected_output: Component using <button> to trigger, role='listbox' with aria-expanded, keyboard handlers for ArrowUp/ArrowDown/Enter/Escape, visible focus indicators, and focus trap when open.

evals.prompt: Audit this login form for accessibility issues: it uses div tags for fields, placeholder text as labels, and a blue submit button with no focus ring.
evals.expected_output: Report: (1) Replace <div> inputs with <input> + <label> elements. (2) Placeholder is not a label — add visible <label> or aria-label. (3) Add focus-visible outline meeting 3:1 contrast. (4) Ensure button text meets 4.5:1 contrast ratio.

evals.prompt: I'm using a red border to show form validation errors. What else do I need to do for accessibility?
evals.expected_output: Never rely on color alone. Add an error icon or text message (e.g. 'This field is required'), link the message to the input via aria-describedby, and use aria-invalid='true' on the invalid field. Announce errors with aria-live='polite'.

should_not_trigger: How do I configure webpack for production builds?
What is the difference between SQL JOIN types?
Set up a Redis cache for a Node.js API
